channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How Endava is redesigning software delivery around AI agents
item.description: Learn how Endava is using AI agents, ChatGPT Enterprise, and Codex to accelerate software delivery, automate workflows, and build an AI-native culture across the enterprise.

item.title: Task-Seeded Synthetic Q&A Generation for Nemotron Pretraining
AI's Finance Problem Is Quantified — And That's Bullish for the Builders
item.description: What Happened BigFinanceBench (928 expert-authored tasks) and Hedge-Bench (102 real hedge-fund analyst tasks) dropped simultaneously, giving the market its first rigorous, rubric-graded...

item.title: Python’s Private Variables Aren't Private: An AppSec Reality Check
item.description: Coming to Python from Java or C++? You might have a dangerous assumption about data encapsulation. Look at this typical snippet used for "secure" state management: class BankAccount: def...

item.title: Runtime Internals: Heap, VMT, and Built-ins | Internals do runtime
item.description: Bilingual post · Post bilíngue Jump to: English · Português English {#english} Runtime Internals: Heap, VMT, and Built-ins Post 037 explained how real .pas units load. Once parsed, something must...

item.title: People Without Dirty Hands Are Wrong
item.description: A Tale of Two Managers Erin Erin was a newly-promoted manager over a software engineering team. She had been hired 5 years ago as a junior developer and worked on 3 major projects in her time with...

item.title: I Built Kyro: An Open-Source CLI for React Native Diagnostics and Repair
item.description: I Built Kyro: An Open-Source CLI for React Native Diagnostics and Repair Have you ever spent hours debugging a React Native project only to discover the issue was something simple? Maybe: Android...

item.title: "Your Webhooks Are Failing Silently — Here's How We Fixed It" published: false
item.description: description: "After reading 50+ Dev.to posts about webhook frustrations, we built HookSniff to solve the 5 biggest problems developers face with webhook delivery." Your Webhooks Are Failing...

item.title: What Token Extensions Finally Made Me Understand
item.description: Subtitle: Token-2022 showed me that tokens can have rules, not just balances. This week I moved past basic SPL tokens and started using Token Extensions. At first, I thought a token was mostly...

item.title: LongTracer: Open-Source RAG Hallucination Detection Without LLM-as-a-Judge
item.description: Stop paying to evaluate your LLM outputs. Stop tolerating non-deterministic quality gates. LongTracer is the MIT-licensed Python library that catches RAG hallucinations at inference time — no API...

item.title: Do Claude Code ao Codex — trocar de ambiente para continuar mudando

item.title: Claude Code에서 Codex로 — 계속 바뀌기 위해 작업 환경까지 바꾼다
item.description: Originally published at moday.me. Building MODAY in public. Claude Code에서 Codex로 — 계속 바뀌기 위해 작업 환경까지 바꾼다 Claude Code로 만든 브랜드를 Codex로 옮겨 본다 MODAY의 꽤 큰 부분은 Claude Code로 만들었다. Shopify 테마 수정, Gelato...

item.title: Hacking Meta’s AI Chatbot
item.description: Hackers are convincing Meta’s AI support chatbot to let them take over other peoples’ accounts: A video posted on X showed the step-by-step process to hack someone’s Instagram account. The hacker...

item.title: How courts are coping with a flood of AI-generated lawsuits
item.description: Most days in her chambers, Judge Maritza Braswell, a federal magistrate judge in Colorado, sifts through stacks of documents written by people without a lawyer. Many of them can’t afford to hire a...

item.title: Jeff Bezos Is Funding a Wild Hunt for the Brain’s ‘Core Algorithm’
item.description: With $500 million in funding and a reported $2.5 billion valuation, Flourish wants to reinvent AI by putting real neurons under the microscope.

item.title: Alpha School’s Ritzy New York City Campus Costs $65,000 a Year—but Isn’t Actually a School
item.description: A homeschooling center in Manhattan is part of the company’s nationwide expansion. Internal documents reveal its strategy: “Opening date > safety.”

item.title: Fake Sites Mimicking Open-Source Tools Rank High on Google to Deliver Malware via TDS
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects to funnel unsuspecting users through a Traffic Distribution System (TDS) and...

item.title: Quantum Computing Is Having Its Public Market Moment
item.description: Quantinuum, a quantum computing startup, is losing millions. Investors want in anyway.

item.title: Hackers Spied on a Stock Exchange Executive's Outlook Mailbox for Five Months
item.description: Unknown attackers spent at least five months inside the Outlook mailbox of a senior executive at a major global stock exchange, copying the inbox out in small, repeated batches and routing it...

item.title: Amazon develops a warehouse robot workers can speak to
item.description: Amazon has announced a new version of its fully autonomous warehouse robot, Proteus, that will can interact using language instead of code. The expanded capabilities come as part of a growing...

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited Magento RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45247 to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical flaw impacting Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento full-page cache extension, to its Known...

item.title: Shokz upgraded its open earbuds with better sound and a lighter design
item.description: Shokz has announced two new versions of its open earbuds. Like the original OpenDots One that launched in May 2025, the new Shokz OpenDots 2 and OpenDots Air are both designed to be worn clipped...

item.title: DoJ Disrupts Southeast Asia Crypto Fraud Networks, Freezes $3.8 Million in Assets
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Wednesday announced the results of a sweeping action undertaken by government authorities and private sector companies to combat cyber-enabled and...

item.title: OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons
item.description: Leading AI labs, executives, and scientists are sending a letter to lawmakers urging them to improve tracking of synthetic DNA sequences that could be used for bioweapons.

item.title: Nintendo confirms it will sell a new Switch 2 with replaceable battery in the EU
item.description: Nintendo is planning to launch versions of Switch 2 hardware in the EU that will let users easily replace the battery. To meet its obligations from a new EU regulation that's set to go into effect...

item.title: Lovable signs multiyear deal with Google Cloud to up usage 5x, source says
item.description: Lovable and Google signed an expanded multiyear deal that involves a 5x expansion of Lovable's footprint on Google Cloud, and expanded access to Anthropic Claude.

item.title: Apple is bringing age verification to Texas this week
item.description: Apple will introduce age verification in the App Store for users in Texas starting on Thursday, June 4th. The move, as spotted by MacRumors, comes just days after a federal appeals court allowed...

item.title: WiiM expands its whole-home ecosystem with a new soundbar
item.description: WiiM, the audio company that's challenged the idea that audiophile-level performance requires a small loan, is expanding its whole-home ecosystem with the WiiM Bar, which releases in July. Much...

item.title: Nvidia is already planning N2X and N3X chips — the goal is the Star Trek computer
item.description: Just in case you were wondering, Nvidia's RTX Spark isn't supposed to be a one-off. The company is not just flirting with becoming the fifth high-profile vendor of consumer laptop chips to see if...

item.title: Can't make sense of Dashlane's vault theft notification? You're not alone.
item.description: Security advisory leaves out key details. Dashlane maintains complete silence.
